task_completion.description: Open the base rocket design in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket application window is open and the file 'motor_sweep.ork' is loaded (visible in the title bar or rocket design tree).

task_completion.description: Conduct a parametric motor study by running multiple simulations with different motors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' or 'Motor configuration' tab in OpenRocket shows multiple simulation rows (at least 7) utilizing different motor designations (at least 4 distinct motors).

task_completion.description: Identify a motor configuration that reaches close to the target apogee of 3048 meters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The simulation results table in OpenRocket shows at least one configuration with an apogee near 3048 meters (within roughly 30%, or ~2100m to ~3900m).

task_completion.description: Export the motor comparison data to a CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'motor_comparison.csv' is visible in the file manager under '/home/ga/Documents/exports/', or the file is open in a spreadsheet/text editor showing exported simulation data.

task_completion.description: Save a motor selection report recommending the optimal motor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text file named 'motor_selection_report.txt' is visible in '/home/ga/Documents/exports/' or open in a text editor, containing a written recommendation for a specific motor to reach the target altitude.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to run the simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with OpenRocket's interface (e.g., motor selection dialogs, simulation run buttons) rather than just writing text files from scratch.

integrity.description: The exported CSV contains authentic simulation data.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the CSV is visible, it contains standard OpenRocket export columns (e.g., Motor, Apogee, Max velocity) and multiple rows of data that align with the simulations run in the GUI.

integrity.description: The recommended motor in the report matches the simulation findings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The motor recommended in 'motor_selection_report.txt' corresponds to one of the high-performing motors visible in the OpenRocket simulation tab or the CSV file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The base rocket is located at /home/ga/Documents/rockets/motor_sweep.ork. The agent must run at least 7 simulations testing a minimum of 4 distinct motors. The target altitude is exactly 3048 meters. The best simulation must reach within 30% of 3048m for full points. The exported CSV must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/exports/motor_comparison.csv and the report to /home/ga/Documents/exports/motor_selection_report.txt. Note: The base rocket is a tiny 18mm model, so reaching 3048m requires the agent to either modify the rocket's motor mount or override physical constraints to fit an impossibly large motor into the small tube.
